key_insight: Randomized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo achieves faster mixing for log-concave targets by using random integration times and velocity resets, with total simulation time scaling like $O(\alpha^{-1/2}\log(\varepsilon^{-1}))$ under an $alpha$-Talagrand condition.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical sampling strategy for high-dimensional Bayesian and uncertainty-quantification workflows where faster convergence to the target distribution reduces computational cost.
method: Simulate continuous-time Hamiltonian dynamics for a random duration.
Reset velocity to an independent Gaussian between trajectories.
Use triangular or exponential integration-time distributions; for general log-concave targets, use triangular times with increasing means.
evidence_signal: The paper provides theoretical convergence guarantees, showing exponential decay in KL divergence under an $alpha$-Talagrand assumption and an $O(\varepsilon^{-1/2})$ total-time guarantee for a more general schedule.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on log-concavity and, for the strongest rate, an $alpha$-Talagrand condition, so performance may be weaker or harder to tune outside these assumptions.
paper_title: Accelerated Mixing Time of Randomized Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
